_meta.description: Glossary of terms used across the dashboard — every acronym, process, material and component you'll see in the explorer, matrix, and company thesis notes. Curated for an investor who's heard 'AI infrastructure' but doesn't yet know the chokepoints. Cross-references in see_also.

categories.process: Process & equipment (litho, dep, etch, ALD, MOCVD, hybrid bonding)
categories.ai-compute: AI silicon (GPU, ASIC, CUDA, accelerator platforms)
categories.networking: Interconnect & fabric (NVLink, IB, Ethernet, OCS, SerDes)
categories.optics: Photonics & optical (CPO, PIC, lasers, substrates, transceivers)
categories.power: Power & cooling (SiC, GaN, HVDC, VRM, CDU, SST)
categories.industry: Industry / value chain (OSAT, ODM, WFE, ATE, IDM)

glossary.definition: Lithography using 13.5nm light from laser-vaporized tin droplets. ASML is the sole maker; Zeiss (optics) + Cymer (light source) are single-source within it. Drives all leading-edge logic; the deepest chokepoint in tech.

Next-gen EUV with 0.55 numerical aperture (vs 0.33). Tools ~$380M each, ship in dozens of crates. Intel 14A first deployment Dec 2025; Samsung receiving for SF2; TSMC is holding back, sticking with 0.33-NA + multipatterning for A14/A16.

ArF immersion (193nm) lithography — the pre-EUV workhorse; still used for ≥7nm and as the only China-accessible advanced tool. ASML, Nikon, Canon. SMIC reached 7nm via DUV multipatterning at low yield.
Deposits one atomic layer at a time for ultra-uniform conformal films. Critical for FinFET→GAAFET (3D nanosheet) transition. ASM International (ASM.AS) is the highest-exposure leader.

Grows III-V semiconductor layers (GaAs, InP, GaN) one atomic layer at a time. AIXTRON has 70-90% share + >85% in GaN; Veeco is the only credible #2. The hidden upstream for every photonics laser AND GaN power device.
Polishes wafers flat between layers. CMP slurry: Fujimi, CMC Materials.
Vertical-fin gate transistor design used at 22nm-3nm logic. Replaces planar FET; surrounded on three sides by gate. Replaced at 2nm by GAAFET.

Transistor architecture replacing FinFET at 2nm+. Gate wraps all 4 sides of stacked nanosheet channels for better control + lower leakage. TSMC N2, Samsung SF2, Intel 18A. Drives ALD demand (more 3D surfaces to coat conformally).

Validating that each die works BEFORE stacking it (e.g. in HBM). The hardest test in the industry — MEMS probe cards contact thousands of 40µm-pitch microbumps. Near-duopoly: FormFactor + Micronics Japan. Nvidia is tightening HBM KGD standards.
Test fixture with thousands of microscopic spring-loaded contacts that touch a die's pads during wafer-level test. FormFactor (FORM) + Micronics Japan dominate MEMS probe cards for HBM.

Tiny mechanical devices made with semiconductor processes — used in oscillators (SiTime), vacuum valves (VAT), probe cards (FormFactor), IMUs (TDK), optical mirrors (Lumentum OCS), and pressure sensors (Sensirion).

TSMC's 2.5D advanced packaging where the GPU die + ~8 HBM stacks sit on a silicon interposer. The gating capacity constraint on Nvidia/AMD AI GPU output since 2023. TSMC outsources overflow to ASE, Amkor, SPIL.

Next-gen advanced packaging on rectangular panels (vs. round wafers) — escapes the geometric waste of CoWoS interposers as AI die sizes grow. Powertech (6239.TW) PiFO is a leading CoWoS-L alternative.

Insulating film between layers in an IC substrate. Ajinomoto (2802.T) has a near-monopoly on the film itself — the original food company's hidden silicon-stack chokepoint. ABF substrates: Ibiden, Shinko, Unimicron, Nan Ya, AT&S.
Direct copper-to-copper + oxide-to-oxide wafer bonding (no solder). Replaces TC-NCF at HBM4E+. Enables <10µm 3D stacking. BESI is the equipment leader, ASMPT the only credible #2.
SK Hynix's HBM stacking process — solder bumps pressed under heat, then molded underfill. Hynix's process-yield edge that put it ahead in HBM.
Samsung/Micron's HBM stacking method — alternating compression and film deposition. Loses share to hybrid bonding at HBM4E+. Hanmi Semiconductor has ~90% of HBM4 TCB equipment.

Stack of DRAM dies vertically connected via TSVs sitting right next to the GPU. Modern AI is bandwidth-bound, so HBM gates throughput. SK Hynix ~62%, Micron ~21%, Samsung ~17%; all three sold out for 2026. Each Blackwell GPU carries 8 stacks.
2026 HBM generation. Adds a logic base die fabbed at TSMC (vs commodity DRAM base) — pulls the foundry into the memory stack and raises the integration bar.
Vertical electrical connection through a stacked die, enabling 3D integration in HBM and advanced packaging.
Latest server DRAM module standards. MRDIMM is the high-bandwidth multi-rank variant for AI hosts (CPU memory alongside the GPU's HBM). Interface chips: Montage (688008.SH), Renesas.
Non-volatile flash storage stacked vertically (200+ layers in 3D NAND). Samsung, SK Hynix, Kioxia (285A.T, JP), SanDisk, Micron, YMTC (China).
Stored 'context' during LLM inference. Lives in HBM; pushed to DRAM/SSD as it grows. Long-context (100M+ tokens) and agentic workflows balloon KV cache → lifts demand across the memory tier.

Google's custom AI accelerator (Broadcom-designed). Systolic-array architecture: data flows through fixed multiplier cells (vs. instructions per cycle on a GPU). Far better perf/watt on ML workloads, useless outside it. Trillium (TPU v7) co-designed with MediaTek.

Custom chip optimized for a specific workload (vs. general-purpose GPU/CPU). Hyperscalers' answer to Nvidia tax: TPU (Google), Trainium (AWS), Maia (Microsoft), MTIA (Meta). Broadcom + Marvell co-design ~95% of merchant custom ASICs; +44.6% projected 2026, ~28% of AI servers.
Nvidia's GPU programming platform — 15+ years of libraries that lock customers in. The deepest software moat in AI. Switching to TPU = also switching framework (PyTorch → JAX).
Multimodal model that converts images + text instructions into robot motor actions. Collapses see/think/move into one stream. Nvidia GR00T, Figure Helix, Physical Intelligence π₀.
Nvidia's open humanoid-scale VLA foundation model + Isaac Sim training pipeline. Lets a robot learn a task from a YouTube video in days. The 'brain' standard for embodied AI.
Nvidia's GB200 rack-scale system with 72 liquid-cooled GPUs interconnected via NVLink. The new unit of AI compute — replaces the single server. Built by Foxconn / Quanta / Wiwynn ODMs.
Smart NIC offloading network + storage from the host CPU directly to the GPUs. Nvidia BlueField, AMD Pensando, Intel IPU.

Chip that monitors + controls a server's health, power, and updates independently of the host CPU. ASPEED (5274.TWO) has ~80% general and ~100% of Nvidia Hopper/Blackwell-rack BMCs (87 per NVL72) — an 'ASML-like' near-monopoly.
Open standard for chiplet-to-chiplet interconnect inside a package. Synopsys + Cadence have UCIe IP.
Nvidia's proprietary high-speed GPU-to-GPU fabric within a node/rack. Massive switching cost / moat. Challenged by open UALink (AMD, Broadcom et al.).
Open scale-up fabric alternative to NVLink, backed by AMD + Broadcom + others. Astera Labs targeting UALink products in 2026.
Scale-out fabric battle for AI clusters: Nvidia Quantum (InfiniBand) vs the open Ultra Ethernet Consortium (Arista, Broadcom). Ethernet gaining at the largest clusters.

MEMS-mirror based optical switching used in Google TPU pods (Project Apollo). Cuts power vs electrical switches at scale. Lumentum supplies the OCS systems (R300/R64); MEMS mirrors fabbed by Silex Microsystems (Sai MicroElectronics 300456.SZ).
High-speed signaling silicon that turns parallel chip I/O into serial wire signals. Running at 112G→224G/lane and rising. Synopsys, Alphawave, Astera Labs.
Silicon that regenerates a degraded high-speed signal (~1m) to extend reach. Astera Labs (PCIe/CXL), Credo. Critical at 224G.

Server-to-server (vs user-to-server 'north-south') data inside the datacenter. Agentic AI generates ~25x chatbot traffic with ~80% east-west; machine-to-machine forecast to grow ~10→537 EB/month by 2034 (Nokia).

Moves optics directly next to the switch/GPU die instead of as pluggable transceivers — cuts per-port power from ~30W to ~9W. Nvidia ships commercial CPO in 2026 (Quantum-X 1H, Spectrum-X 2H) on TSMC COUPE; Broadcom Tomahawk 6 Davisson. FAU + optics block made by Himax + FOCI for COUPE.
Integrates lasers, modulators, waveguides, photodetectors on one chip. Built on SOI (silicon photonics) or InP (compound semiconductor) substrates.

Hardware that couples a bundle of fibers to a photonic chip. For TSMC's COUPE platform: Himax (HIMX) stamps the nanoimprint optics block + V-groove baseplate via wafer-level optics; FOCI (3363.TWO) assembles the FAU.
TSMC's silicon-photonics packaging platform for CPO. 3D stacked; targets 1.6T+. Runs on SOI substrate (Soitec); FAU from Himax + FOCI.

High-speed datacom laser — the workhorse of 800G/1.6T modules. <5 makers at scale: Lumentum, Coherent, Mitsubishi Electric, Sumitomo, Broadcom. Nvidia committed ~$4B (~$2B each to LITE+COHR) to lock supply. Lead times past 2027.
Single-wavelength laser used in datacom + telecom. Furukawa Electric (5801.T) raising DFB-chip capacity +500% by 2028; Nvidia locked long-term contracts.

External light source feeding a silicon-photonics modulator (vs. EML which modulates the laser itself). The CPO future: market mix shifts from 76% EML / 24% CW-LD (2024) → 31% EML / 69% CW-LD (2028) per Sumitomo Electric.
Surface-emitting laser used in short-reach optical interconnects + 3D-sensing (Face ID, depth cameras). ams OSRAM, Lumentum.
Building optical components on silicon wafers (vs InP). Lower cost + foundry-process-compatible; needs external lasers (CW-LD). TSMC COUPE, GFS Fotonix, UMC iSiPP300, Tower Semi.
Substrate with a thin silicon layer on an insulating oxide buried under it — every silicon photonic IC is built on SOI. Soitec (SOI.PA) ~80% share; Shin-Etsu licenses its Smart Cut technology.
Soitec's patented SOI fabrication process — ion implantation + wafer bonding + thermal split. Decades-long moat; even Shin-Etsu licenses it.

III-V compound-semiconductor substrate for high-performance lasers/PICs (EML, DFB, modulators). Capacity expanding: Sumitomo (5802.T) 2.4x by 2028; Coherent tripled; Nokia (NOK) owns the only vertically-integrated InP fab in the West (ex-Infinera).
Next-gen modulator substrate enabling 200G+/lane. Soitec extending into LNOI via Smart Cut.

Standalone optical module that plugs into a switch front panel (the standard for 10+ years). 800G+ shipments ~24M (2025) → ~63M (2026). China dominates assembly (~63%; InnoLight + Eoptolink ~60% of Nvidia 800G).

Wide-bandgap semiconductor for high-voltage power conversion. ~3x copper's thermal conductivity. AI is 'inheriting' the EV/solar-built SiC supply chain. Wolfspeed (WOLF) only commercial 200mm + first 300mm SiC crystal; SICC (688234.SH) China substrate leader.
Wide-bandgap semiconductor for high-frequency power conversion (48V→core, AI rack VRMs). MOCVD-grown — AIXTRON has >85% share for GaN tools. Pure-plays: Navitas (NVTS), Transphorm (Renesas).
Rack architecture replacing legacy 54V; the only path to 600kW+ racks. Wide-bandgap silicon goes from ~0% to ~64% of rack power BOM. Nvidia's 29-member 800VDC alliance announced at COMPUTEX 2025.

Steps intermediate bus voltage down to GPU core voltage (~0.7V at ~3,500A for Rubin Ultra — 'the thousand-amp problem'). Multi-phase: dozens of channels per GPU. MPWR is Nvidia's reference VRM incumbent; Vicor (factorized power), Renesas (GaN VRMs).

SiC-based replacement for century-old iron-core transformers (whose lead times have stretched to 18-30 months). Eaton, ABB, Siemens, Hitachi Energy, GE Vernova racing for 2027. Private pure-plays: Heron Power, DG Matrix.

Distributes coolant to liquid-cooled racks. Capacity is currently the cooling bottleneck (market ~$1B→$7.7B as liquid cooling jumps from ~15% (2024) to ~76% of mix by 2026). Vertiv leads; Auras (3324.TWO), AVC (3017.TW).
Power-quality discrete component. Murata, Samsung Electro-Mechanics dominate.
High-torque reduction gear for mid-large industrial robot joints. Japan's Nabtesco (6268.T) ~60% share. The high-torque cousin of the harmonic reducer.

High-precision, high-ratio reduction gear used in robot wrist/elbow joints. Japan's Harmonic Drive (6324.T) near-monopoly; China's Leaderdrive / Green Harmonic (688017.SH) ~25%+ challenger targeting Optimus supply.
Lower-cost rotary reduction gear — the all-around workhorse. Apex Dynamics (4583.TW, Taiwan) is biggest after Germany's Wittenstein.

Linear-motion mechanism with ~5x ball-screw life — the humanoid linear-actuator bottleneck. NSK, THK, GSA (Germany — primary Optimus supplier), Rollvis, Ewellix; China second-sources: Beite (603009.SH, ¥1.85bn plant), Ningbo Shuanglin (300100.SZ, 300K/yr for Optimus).
Direct-drive ring motor with hollow core, integrated into a joint without separate housing. Kollmorgen (now Regal Rexnord), Parker-Hannifin, Allient (ALNT). Optimus integrates ~28 kits.

Six-axis sensor measuring forces + torques at robot wrists/ankles for dexterous manipulation. Critical for tactile control. ATI (Novanta NOVT), Bota Systems, Sunrise Instruments, Keli Sensing (603662.SH).
Accelerometer + gyroscope (sometimes magnetometer) sensing motion + orientation. Critical for balance/locomotion. TDK InvenSense (ICM-42688 widely used in humanoids), Bosch, STMicro, Analog Devices.
4-axis industrial pick-and-place arm — fast, accurate, dominant in electronics assembly. Seiko Epson (6724.T) is #1.
Electronically-commutated DC motor — no brushes; long life; precise control. Inside every robotic joint motor.
Reflective micro-display tech — mature, lower cost, lower brightness. Inside the Meta Ray-Ban Display ($799, Sept 2025). Himax (HIMX), Omnivision (Will Semi).
OLED micro-display on a silicon backplane — premium VR/MR brightness. Sony (6758.T) supplies Apple Vision Pro; BOE (000725.SZ), SeeYa.
Self-emissive micro-display — brightest option for daylight AR, but lowest yield. JBD (China, dominant), Porotech (UK), AUO (2409.TW), Playnitride, ams OSRAM. Meta Orion uses full-color microLED.

Thin slab of high-index glass or SiC etched with gratings, piping the projector's light into the eye. Reflective: Lumus (in Meta Ray-Ban Display); diffractive: Dispelix, DigiLens, WaveOptics (Snap), Vuzix. Low light-efficiency = the AR-optics chokepoint.
Mechanical pattern transfer via a stamp (vs photolithography). Used for waveguide gratings and CPO FAU optics blocks. Tools: EV Group, Canon, SUSS MicroTec.
Lens manufacturing at wafer scale via nanoimprint — Himax goes back to iPhone X Face ID dot-projector optics for this. Now repurposed for CPO FAU + AR waveguide gratings.
Companies that handle the back-end of chip manufacturing — packaging, bonding, testing. ASE (3711.TW, world #1), Amkor, JCET (China), Powertech (6239.TW), KYEC (test), Tongfu.
Capital equipment that goes into a fab. Big-5 (ASML/AMAT/LRCX/KLAC/TEL) ~70% of the ~$110B+/yr market. China was ~30-40% of spend during the localization rush.
Test equipment for chips post-fabrication. Duopoly: Advantest (6857.T) + Teradyne (TER). HBM/AI complexity lengthens test time → direct AI test-demand leverage.

Designs + builds products on contract for brand customers. Taiwan ODMs build the world's AI servers: Foxconn (2317.TW), Quanta (2382.TW), Wiwynn (6669.TW, ~50% of hyperscale AI-server procurement), Wistron, Inventec.
Company that both designs and fabricates its own chips (Intel, Samsung, STMicro, Infineon). Contrast with fabless (Nvidia, AMD, Broadcom) + foundry (TSMC).
